# Vendored reStructuredText specification

`spec.rst` is the [reStructuredText Markup Specification](https://docutils.sourceforge.io/docs/ref/rst/restructuredtext.html),
authored by David Goodger and the Docutils contributors. The document has been **placed in the
public domain** by its authors, so it carries no usage restrictions; the full Docutils public-domain
dedication is reproduced in `LICENSE` (the project's `COPYING.rst`).

It is the reStructuredText source form of the published specification page, vendored verbatim. The
specification has no version number of its own; it ships with Docutils, so `VERSION` records the
Docutils release it was taken from (`1.0b1.dev`). Because the GitHub mirror carries no release tags
and the published page tracks the development branch, the exact pin is the commit
`3feeda18a3140d4444ece52c1d3def99a0783316`.

It is vendored here as a public format specification — an allowed source of truth for this
clean-room implementation — to document the markup surface the reStructuredText reader parses. The
embedded markup examples are used as **inputs** only; expected output is regenerated by the pinned
oracle binary, never taken from the document's rendered HTML.

Refresh or re-pin with `tools/fetch-rst-spec.sh` (edit `SPEC_COMMIT` to bump).
